ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -A option Each name is an associative array variable (see Arrays ).
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Each name is an associative array variable (see Arrays ).

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -f option Each name refers to a shell function.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Each name refers to a shell function.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -i option The variable is to be treated as an integer; arithmetic evaluation (see Shell Arithmetic ) is performed when the variable is assigned a value.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: The variable is to be treated as an integer; arithmetic evaluation (see Shell Arithmetic ) is performed when the variable is assigned a value.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -l option When the variable is assigned a value, all upper-case characters are converted to lower-case.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: When the variable is assigned a value, all upper-case characters are converted to lower-case.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -n option Give each name the nameref attribute, making it a name reference to another variable.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Give each name the nameref attribute, making it a name reference to another variable.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -r option These names cannot then be assigned values by subsequent assignment statements or unset.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: These names cannot then be assigned values by subsequent assignment statements or unset.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -t option Give each name the trace attribute.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Give each name the trace attribute.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -u option When the variable is assigned a value, all lower-case characters are converted to upper-case.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: When the variable is assigned a value, all lower-case characters are converted to upper-case.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -x option Mark each name for export to subsequent commands via the environment.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Mark each name for export to subsequent commands via the environment.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -d option Display a short description of each pattern.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Display a short description of each pattern.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -m option Display the description of each pattern in a manpage-like format.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Display the description of each pattern in a manpage-like format.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -s option Display only a short usage synopsis for each pattern.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Display only a short usage synopsis for each pattern.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -d option Use the first character of delim to terminate each input line, rather than newline.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Use the first character of delim to terminate each input line, rather than newline.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -n option If count is 0, copy all lines.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: If count is 0, copy all lines.

ir_specs.statement: The `bash builtins` -O option Begin assigning to array at index origin .
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Begin assigning to array at index origin .
